deviations.description: The control flow constraint was changed from requiring 'obtain patient consent' before 'conduct time out procedure' to requiring 'obtain patient consent' before 'surgical intervention'. The process model already enforces that consent precedes surgery, so this change has no negative impact. However, the original requirement that 'obtain patient consent' must precede 'conduct time out procedure' is no longer present, and the new requirement introduces a stricter resource constraint for the 'time out procedure' (see below).

The new requirement introduces a resource constraint that 'obtain patient consent' must be performed by a nurse. The process model assigns 'Ask Patient for Consent' to the Nurse lane, so this is already satisfied and does not introduce non-compliance.

The new requirement adds a control flow constraint that after 'obtain patient consent', a 'time out procedure' must follow. The process model already includes 'Perform Time Out' after consent, so this is satisfied.

The new requirement introduces a resource constraint that the 'time out procedure' must be performed by a doctor. In the process model, 'Perform Time Out' is assigned to the Nurse lane, not the Doctor lane. This results in non-compliance because the process does not satisfy the stricter resource constraint.
